title: Currently buildah run is not handling command options correctly
body:
This patch will allow commands like

buildah run $ctr ls -lZ /

To work correctly.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit 86513dc has been approved by `TomSweeneyRedHat`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=TomSweeneyRedHat 86513dce4f3837307d4783633ba6a57a8ff9b0aa -->
"Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest: unauthorized: authentication required" -  fedoraproject.org needs authentication now?

:pushpin: Commit 67c4605 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 67c4605ed029f23b465d6eac9e0eb2b489951e5e -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

FWIW I was previously doing:

`buildah config ${container} --entrypoint blah` etc., and that broke after this PR.

But eh, after reading now I understand why the change was made.
(It's tricky since the rest of my code is lots of `buildah run ${container} arguments...`)
